chunk_summary: India’s lactation management network uses a hub-and-spoke model integrating donor milk banking, lactation support, and referral pathways across CLMCs, LMUs, LMCs, and LSUs, ensuring safe handling, cold-chain integrity, and traceability of expressed and donor milk.
learning_objectives: Understand the roles of CLMCs, LMUs, LMCs, and LSUs in India’s lactation management network.
Learn the processes for donor milk screening, pasteurization, storage, and distribution.
Identify referral pathways and cold-chain protocols for safe inter-facility milk transport.
Explore criteria for donor human milk eligibility and usage duration.
key_facts: Comprehensive Lactation Management Centres (CLMCs) perform Holder pasteurization and microbiology release before distributing donor human milk (DHM).
Lactation Management Units (LMUs) focus on mother–infant dyad support and handle expressed breast milk (EBM) storage and dispensing.
Cold-chain integrity and traceability are critical, requiring validated insulated transport and chain-of-custody documentation.
Referral pathways link smaller facilities (LMCs, LSUs) to CLMCs for DHM when clinically indicated.
India’s lactation network operates under national guidelines, ensuring regulated donor milk banking and breastfeeding support.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Comprehensive Lactation Management Centres (CLMC) are tertiary milk banks that recruit and screen donors, perform Holder pasteurization, and distribute pasteurized donor human milk (DHM) for eligible infants. — National guidelines on lactation management centres
